core_mechanism: an effective wave-trapping horizon formed where a moving background medium's local flow speed crosses the local signal (wave) propagation speed, so excitations cannot cross from the supercritical to the subcritical region and are emitted with a thermal spectrum via mode conversion at that critical surface
brief_reason: The abstract describes a stationary inhomogeneous flow in which the medium's velocity exceeds the group velocity of the waves, trapping them and producing thermal (Hawking) emission at the transition surface, which is a generic transonic critical-surface horizon in an advected wave field.
